name: Perplexity AI API
what_it_does: Perplexity AI provides a search-augmented LLM API (Sonar models) that grounds responses in real-time web search results with citations, alongside standard offline LLM inference, via an OpenAI-compatible REST API.
use_cases: Build agents that need up-to-date factual answers grounded in current web content without implementing a separate search-and-retrieval pipeline
Generate cited research summaries by sending queries to the Sonar model and parsing the returned citations array for source attribution
Answer time-sensitive questions (current events, recent product releases, live pricing) where training data cutoffs would produce stale responses
Implement a lightweight research assistant that combines web retrieval and synthesis in a single API call instead of separate search + LLM steps
Power FAQ systems that need to stay current without re-training by routing factual queries through Sonar for live-grounded answers
not_for: Tasks where internet access to external sources is prohibited by compliance policy or where answers must be limited to a controlled knowledge base
High-throughput batch processing of purely internal documents where web search adds latency and cost without benefit
Applications requiring strict determinism or reproducibility where the same query must return identical outputs every time
best_when: You need an LLM that can answer questions about current events, recent information, or any topic where freshness and cited sources are more important than pure reasoning depth.
avoid_when: Your use case involves proprietary internal knowledge, requires deterministic outputs, or is a workload where web search results are irrelevant or harmful to accuracy.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header. OpenAI SDK compatible — set base_url to api.perplexity.ai and api_key to use existing OpenAI client code against Sonar endpoints.

pricing.paid_tiers: Sonar (online search): ~$5/1000 requests + $1/M tokens. Sonar Pro (deep research): ~$5/1000 requests + $3/M tokens. Sonar Reasoning (with search): ~$5/1000 requests + $5/M tokens. Offline models (sonar-chat): ~$0.20/M input + $0.60/M output tokens.
pricing.notes: Search-enabled models include a per-request fee on top of token costs to cover real-time web retrieval. Number of search queries per request is not user-controllable. Pricing is higher than pure inference providers but includes built-in retrieval.

performance.rate_limits: Default 50 RPM for new accounts. Token-per-minute limits not prominently published. Search-augmented models have higher latency than offline models due to retrieval step.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAI-compatible error envelope with type and message. Search failures and retrieval timeouts surface as model-level errors rather than distinct error codes, making it hard for agents to distinguish search failures from inference failures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each request triggers a new real-time web search; identical queries can return different results if web content has changed. No idempotency keys or response caching.
known_agent_gotchas: The citations array in responses requires post-processing to extract and attribute sources; the citation indices in response text ([1], [2]) must be mapped to the citations list manually
Search-augmented models have higher and more variable latency (2-8s) than pure LLM inference; agents with strict timeout budgets must set generous deadlines for Sonar calls
The model cannot be instructed to skip web search for a given query — online models always search, which can surface irrelevant web content for queries better answered from training data
Web search results introduce non-determinism; the same question on different days or even different API calls may retrieve different sources and produce different answers
Rate limits are low (50 RPM default) compared to other LLM APIs; agents that need high-throughput search-grounded generation will quickly hit limits and require a tier upgrade

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Single API key with no scope restrictions. Search-augmented models send query content to Perplexity's search infrastructure — agents handling sensitive queries should use offline models instead. GDPR compliance noted but no public SOC 2 documentation.
